summary: SHAP Summary Plot fix verified: Bars now properly diverge from zero reference line. Pink bars (positive push) extend RIGHT from zero, cyan bars (negative pull) extend LEFT from zero. The fix removed stackId='a' from both Bar components in ExplainabilityView.js lines 151-152, allowing grouped bars instead of stacked bars. All regression tests passed.

issues:
Minor: validateDOMNesting warning -
cannot appear as descendant of

in Badge component (pre-existing issue)

critical_code_review_comments: FIX VERIFIED (ExplainabilityView.js lines 151-152): Removed stackId='a' from both Bar components in SHAP Summary Plot
Line 151: <Bar dataKey="positive" fill="#ec4899" radius={[0, 4, 4, 0]} name="Positive push" isAnimationActive={false} />
Line 152: <Bar dataKey="negative" fill="#06b6d4" radius={[4, 0, 0, 4]} name="Negative pull" isAnimationActive={false} />
Without stackId, bars render as grouped bars that independently start from zero, properly showing divergence
context_for_next_testing_agent: SHAP Summary Plot fix is verified. The Insurance Costs sample dataset (1100 rows, 9 columns) was used for testing. Training completes in ~1-2 seconds (cached). The chart now shows: (1) Pink bars extending RIGHT from x=0 for positive SHAP values, (2) Cyan bars extending LEFT from x=0 for negative SHAP values, (3) Dashed reference line at x=0, (4) Legend showing 'Increases prediction' and 'Decreases prediction'.

SHAP_SUMMARY_PLOT_FIX.description: SHAP Summary Plot bars should properly diverge from zero reference line instead of being stacked
SHAP_SUMMARY_PLOT_FIX.root_cause: Recharts stackId='a' on both Bar components caused negative bars to start from end of positive bars instead of from zero
SHAP_SUMMARY_PLOT_FIX.fix_applied: Removed stackId='a' from both Bar components in ExplainabilityView.js lines 151-152
